children.heading: Regulations providing exclusions from term
children.label: b Regulations providing exclusions from term
children.text: Nothing in the regulations prescribed for purposes of chapter 24 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (relating to income tax withholding) which provides an exclusion from “wages” as used in such chapter shall be construed to require a similar exclusion from “wages” in the regulations prescribed for purposes of this subchapter.

children.heading: Individuals performing domestic services
children.label: c Individuals performing domestic services
children.text: For purposes of this subchapter, in the case of domestic service described in subsection (a)(6)(B), any payment of cash remuneration for such service which is more or less than a whole-dollar amount shall, under such conditions and to such extent as may be prescribed by regulations made under this subchapter, be computed to the nearest dollar. For the purpose of the computation to the nearest dollar, the payment of a fractional part of a dollar shall be disregarded unless it amounts to one-half dollar or more, in which case it shall be increased to $1. The amount of any payment of cash remuneration so computed to the nearest dollar shall, in lieu of the amount actually paid, be deemed to constitute the amount of cash remuneration for purposes of subsection (a)(6)(B).

children.heading: Members of uniformed services
children.label: d Members of uniformed services
children.text: For purposes of this subchapter, in the case of an individual performing service, as a member of a uniformed service, to which the provisions of section 410()(1) of this title are applicable, the term “wages” shall, subject to the provisions of subsection (a)(1) of this section, include as such individual’s remuneration for such service only (1) his basic pay as described in chapter 3 and in the case of an individual performing service to which subparagraph (A) of such section 410()(1) of this title applies, or (2) his compensation for such service as determined under in the case of an individual performing service to which subparagraph (B) of such section 410()(1) of this title applies.lllsection 1009 of title 37section 206(a) of title 37

children.heading: Peace Corps volunteers
children.label: e Peace Corps volunteers
children.text: For purposes of this subchapter, in the case of an individual performing service, as a volunteer or volunteer leader within the meaning of the Peace Corps Act [ et seq.], to which the provisions of section 410() of this title are applicable, (1) the term “wages” shall, subject to the provisions of subsection (a) of this section, include as such individual’s remuneration for such service only amounts certified as payable pursuant to section 5(c) or 6(1) of the Peace Corps Act [ or 2505(1)], and (2) any such amount shall be deemed to have been paid to such individual at the time the service, with respect to which it is paid, is performed.22 U.S.C. 250122 U.S.C. 2504(c)o

children.heading: Tips
children.label: f Tips
children.text: For purposes of this subchapter, tips received by an employee in the course of his employment shall be considered remuneration for employment. Such remuneration shall be deemed to be paid at the time a written statement including such tips is furnished to the employer pursuant to section 6053(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 or (if no statement including such tips is so furnished) at the time received.

children.heading: Members of religious orders
children.label: g Members of religious orders
children.text: For purposes of this subchapter, in any case where an individual is a member of a religious order (as defined in section 3121(r)(2) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986) performing service in the exercise of duties required by such order, and an election of coverage under section 3121(r) of such Code is in effect with respect to such order or with respect to the autonomous subdivision thereof to which such member belongs, the term “wages” shall, subject to the provisions of subsection (a) of this section, include as such individual’s remuneration for such service the fair market value of any board, lodging, clothing, and other perquisites furnished to such member by such order or subdivision thereof or by any other person or organization pursuant to an agreement with such order or subdivision, except that the amount included as such individual’s remuneration under this paragraph shall not be less than $100 a month.

children.heading: Retired justices and judges
children.label: h Retired justices and judges
children.text: For purposes of this subchapter, in the case of an individual performing service under the provisions of (relating to assignment of retired justices and judges to active duty), the term “wages” shall not include any payment under section 371(b) of such title 28 which is received during the period of such service.section 294 of title 28

children.label: i Employer contributions under sections 401(k) and 414(h)(2) of Internal Revenue Code

children.text: Any employer contribution under a qualified cash or deferred arrangement (as defined in section 401(k) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986) to the extent not included in gross income by reason of section 402(a)(8) of such Code, or

Any amount which is treated as an employer contribution under section 414(h)(2) of such Code where the pickup referred to in such section is pursuant to a salary reduction agreement (whether evidenced by a written instrument or otherwise).

children.heading: Amounts deferred under nonqualified deferred compensation plans
children.label: j Amounts deferred under nonqualified deferred compensation plans
children.text: Any amount deferred under a nonqualified deferred compensation plan (within the meaning of section 3121(v)(2)(C) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986) shall be taken into account for purposes of this subchapter as of the later of when the services are performed, or when there is no substantial risk of forfeiture of the rights to such amount. Any amount taken into account as wages by reason of the preceding sentence (and the income attributable thereto) shall not thereafter be treated as wages for purposes of this subchapter.
